articles.title: NATE DIAZ RETURNS vs MIKE PERRY?! + UFC London Preview!
articles.description: Nate Diaz is officially BACK in MMA and he’s taking on Mike Perry in a wild matchup that honestly nobody saw coming. After leaving the UFC in 2022, Diaz boxed Jake Paul and beat Jorge Masvidal, but now returns at 40 against one of the most dangerous and unpredictable fighters outside the promotion. Perry has been on a tear in bare knuckle, smashing names like Luke Rockhold and Michael “Venom” Page, but if this fight hits the ground, Diaz has a huge advantage. I break down who wins, what to expect, and why the only fight that really makes sense next is Diaz vs Conor McGregor 3. Plus, a full preview of UFC London including Movsar Evloev vs Lerone Murphy and Michael Venom Page vs Sam Patterson. Let me know your predictions in the comments. 🔒 Become a Member for special Perks, Exclusive Content and ultra-exclusive Live Chats: https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCDrG2_1TcVkXKXXsD6Kjwig/join #bisping #ufc #mma #mixedmartialarts #michaelbisping #ufcnews #mmanews #ufclondon #netflixsports #rouseycarano #natediaz

articles.title: Pereira Was a Great Matchup for Jones #ufc
articles.description: Stylistically, this was the fight. Jon Jones vs Alex Pereira wasn’t just big — it made perfect sense. On the feet, Jones is one of the most deceptive strikers we’ve ever seen. He’s a trick fighter. He keeps you guessing. He attacks everywhere — knees, legs, body, head. You don’t just fight Jones. You get picked apart by him. But the real danger? If Jon gets on top of you even once. That’s where fights change. And for Pereira — a world-class striker with limited wrestling background — that’s the biggest question. Can he stop the takedown? Can he survive the ground? Because if he can’t, the fight tilts hard. Now add the stakes: A championship fight — even if interim — puts Jones right back in position. A win likely leads to a showdown with Tom Aspinall. Massive money. Historic stage. Career-defining moment. Everything lined up. The matchup. The timing. The opportunity. But it didn’t happen. Jones passed. And now the conversation shifts from “what could happen” to “what could have been.” Because this wasn’t just another fight — this was a pathway: To another belt. To another payday. To another legacy moment. And those windows don’t stay open forever. #UFC #MMA #JonJones #AlexPereira #TomAspinall #FightBreakdown #MMANews #Heavyweight #CombatSports #GOAT

articles.title: The Man Who COULD Beat Chimaev #ufc
articles.description: Let’s be honest — to even imagine Khamzat Chimaev losing, you have to solve one problem first: Can anyone stop the takedown? Because if you can’t… the fight’s over before it really starts. Chimaev’s offensive wrestling is suffocating. Once he gets his hands on you, it’s control, pressure, and damage. That’s the puzzle every contender has to crack. So who actually has the skill set to make us believe? Who has the takedown defense, composure, and mindset to survive those early moments? I think it’s Sean Strickland. Not because he’s flashy. Not because he’s perfect. But because of how he fights. Strickland stays disciplined. He controls distance. He doesn’t panic. And most importantly — he doesn’t break. That matters against a guy like Chimaev, who thrives on overwhelming opponents fast. If someone can force him into deeper waters, extend the fight, and make him work… now you’ve got a real contest. Is it easy? Not even close. But if you’re building the blueprint to beat Chimaev, it starts with takedown defense and mental toughness. Strickland checks those boxes. And that’s why this matchup isn’t just interesting — it might be necessary. Because until someone proves they can stop that wrestling, Chimaev remains one of the most dangerous problems in MMA. #UFC #MMA #KhamzatChimaev #SeanStrickland #FightBreakdown #MMANews #Wrestling #CombatSports #Middleweight #FightAnalysis

articles.title: Why Topuria vs Makhachev Didn’t Happen #ufc
articles.description: The superfight everyone wanted just hit a wall. Ilia Topuria vs Islam Makhachev didn’t happen — and now we’re finally hearing why. From Islam’s side, the explanation is simple: Ilia left the division. Fair enough. But then it got more complicated. Islam claims Ilia agreed to the fight… and then put a price tag on it that didn’t make sense. Too high. Unrealistic. A deal-breaker. Islam’s response? “I’ll fight you for free.” That’s not negotiation — that’s a statement. That’s a fighter saying it’s not about money… it’s about proving a point. So where does that leave things? For now, the opportunity moves on. Justin Gaethje steps into position. The division keeps moving. The belt keeps moving. That’s how the UFC works. You don’t wait — you replace. But here’s the key: this story isn’t over. If anything, it’s just getting started. Because now there’s tension. Now there’s public disagreement. Now there’s something personal. Ilia vs Islam isn’t dead — it’s delayed. And delayed fights with real friction? They get bigger. The stakes grow. The narrative builds. The payoff gets massive. Right now, it’s Gaethje’s moment. But don’t lose track of the bigger picture. Because when Ilia and Islam finally meet… It won’t just be about a belt. It’ll be about settling something real. #UFC #MMA #IliaTopuria #IslamMakhachev #JustinGaethje #FightNews #MMARumors #Lightweight #CombatSports #Superfight

articles.title: Pereira Wins… But Does It Count? #ufc
articles.description: Here’s the uncomfortable truth about interim titles: Most fans don’t care. You might. I might. But the broader audience? They don’t treat an interim belt like the real thing. And that’s the problem. If Alex Pereira goes to the White House card and wins an interim championship… does he get full credit? Probably not. Fair or not, interim champs live in a weird space: • They’re technically champions • But not universally recognized as the champion • And often need one more fight to prove it That creates a perception gap. Pereira could go out, beat a top contender in a new weight class, and still hear, “Yeah, but it’s interim.” That’s not a knock on him — it’s the system. Now, from a competitive standpoint, it still matters. It moves you into position. It locks in a unification fight. It gets you closer to undisputed gold. And for a guy chasing legacy, that path is everything. But let’s not pretend it carries the same weight publicly. Even behind the scenes, there’s debate. People will tell you to slow down, not to call him “champ” just yet. Because in this sport, the word “champion” is reserved for one thing: Undisputed. So if Pereira wins, it’s big. It’s meaningful. It’s necessary. But until he unifies? There will always be an asterisk in the eyes of the fans. #UFC #MMA #AlexPereira #InterimBelt #UFCTitle #FightNews #CombatSports #Championship #MMAAnalysis

articles.title: Nate Diaz vs Mike Perry — The Netflix Fight Card Explained
articles.description: Nate Diaz vs Mike Perry is officially happening — and the fight comes with a major twist. The bout is scheduled for five rounds, which could completely change the dynamic between the two fighters. Mike Perry has become the face of bare-knuckle boxing, one of the toughest sports in combat. Meanwhile, Nate Diaz has spent years sharpening his boxing skills and remains one of the most durable fighters in the game. But the bigger story might be the Netflix fight card itself. With names like: * Nate Diaz * Mike Perry * Francis Ngannou * Ronda Rousey * Gina Carano Jake Paul has even suggested this event proves the UFC era is ending. Chael Sonnen breaks down: * Why Diaz vs Perry is harder to predict than people think * Why the five-round format matters * The massive star power of Ronda Rousey * The difference between the Netflix fight model and the UFC business model * And the big risk that every new fight promotion faces. 0:00 Nate Diaz vs Mike Perry Fight Announcement 0:35 Mike Perry’s Bare-Knuckle Boxing Resurgence 0:54 Diaz and Perry’s Common Opponent: Jake Paul 1:20 Why Diaz vs Perry Isn’t a Simple Fight 2:00 The Five-Round Negotiation Mystery 2:50 Breaking Down the Netflix Fight Card 3:12 Jake Paul Says the UFC Is Over 3:32 Why the Netflix Card Relies on UFC Stars 4:23 Netflix vs UFC Business Models 5:10 Why Ronda Rousey Is Such a Huge Draw 5:46 Ranking Combat Sports’ Biggest Stars 6:26 Ronda’s Surprising Return Appearance 7:16 The Risk of a Thin Fight Card 8:03 Why It’s Too Early to Crown Netflix the Winner Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: What REALLY Happened When Strickland Trained With Chimaev?
articles.description: Sean Strickland and Khamzat Chimaev trained together in Las Vegas… but the story of what happened in the gym depends on who you ask. Chimaev says he got the better of Strickland.Strickland says that’s not how it went down. So what actually happened? Normally there’s a code in MMA: what happens in the gym stays in the gym. But once both fighters start talking about it publicly, the question becomes obvious… Where are the witnesses? Extreme Couture is a packed room. Fighters, coaches, and training partners were there. Yet nobody has stepped forward to confirm either version of the story. Chael Sonnen breaks down: * why gym stories are notoriously unreliable * how “the mask” works in fighting * why training rounds can be deceptive * and why the Strickland vs Chimaev narrative may be missing key details With their potential fight looming, the mystery of those training sessions only adds more intrigue. 0:00 Strickland vs Chimaev Training Story 0:35 The Code of the Training Room 1:05 Why No One From the Gym Is Talking 1:43 Chimaev’s Media Mystery 2:36 Chimaev Training Footage Surfaces 3:32 Why Strickland Got the Fight 3:48 Dricus Du Plessis Weighs In 4:22 Is Strickland the Better Striker? 5:10 The “Mask” Fighters Wear 6:45 Why Training Rounds Can Be Misleading 7:12 Who Really Saw the Sparring? articles.title: Did Jon Jones Just Make the Biggest Mistake of His Career?
articles.description: Did Jon Jones just walk away from a massive payday? Reports say the UFC offered Jones $15 million for a fight against Alex Pereira — a matchup many believed favored the longtime champion. Instead, Jones reportedly turned it down while dealing with hip arthritis and has now challenged the UFC to release him if they believe he’s finished. Meanwhile, interim heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall continues waiting for a title unification fight. After years of inactivity and ongoing contract drama, the UFC heavyweight division may be ready to move on. Is Jon Jones really done in the UFC… or is this all part of a bigger negotiation? 👇 Let us know in the comments. #UFC #JonJones #MMA #UFCNews #MMAWeekly

articles.title: Did Jon Jones Just Make the Worst Decision of His Career?
articles.description: Jon Jones had a massive opportunity in front of him. A fight with Alex Pereira. A championship on the line. A spot on the historic White House card. And a payday reportedly worth $15 million. But Jon Jones said no. So the real question now is simple: Is Jon Jones in a better position today than he would have been if he accepted the deal? Chael breaks down: • Why Pereira was actually a favorable matchup for Jones • The negotiation mistake fighters sometimes make• How saying “no” can cause the entire deal to disappear• Why the interim heavyweight title changes everything• And how Jones could end up completely iced out of the heavyweight title picture Plus — the scenario where Pereira beats Gane, fights Aspinall… and Jon Jones may never get another shot. 0:00 Jon Jones Turns Down UFC Offer 1:20 Jon Jones vs Alex Pereira Matchup 2:30 White House Card Opportunity 3:10 Jon Jones Social Media Reaction 4:40 $15 Million UFC Payday 6:10 Interim Title & Tom Aspinall Situation 7:40 Is Jon Jones Better Off? articles.title: Francis Ngannou: “Keep the Legacy, I’ll Take the Money”
articles.description: Francis Ngannou made an interesting statement during a recent MVP press conference: "Keep the legacy… I’ll take the money." For Chael Sonnen, it was one of the most refreshing comments he’s heard in combat sports. Fighters spend years talking about their legacy, but Chael argues that legacy is something no fighter can actually control. It’s simply what people say about you long after your career is over. Ngannou’s point wasn’t that he literally doesn’t care — it’s that he understands legacy isn’t something you can manage. And when you look at Francis’ career, the idea that he avoids difficult fights doesn’t hold up. Ngannou has already fought some of the toughest names in combat sports: * Cain Velasquez * Stipe Miocic (twice) * Tyson Fury * Anthony Joshua He even agreed to fights that never happened — including a potential matchup with Rico Verhoeven. Chael explains why Ngannou’s attitude toward legacy may actually be the most honest approach a fighter can take. Because legacy isn’t an opponent. And it’s not something you can fight. Timestamps 0:00 The Francis Ngannou quote everyone is talking about 0:18 “Keep the legacy, I’ll take the money” 0:45 Does Francis really not care about legacy? 1:10 What fighters actually mean when they say that 1:45 Why legacy is something you can’t control 2:05 The Jon Jones narrative around Francis 2:45 Looking back at Ngannou’s record 3:00 The Cain Velasquez fight people forget 3:25 Fighting Stipe Miocic twice 3:45 Boxing Tyson Fury and Anthony Joshua 4:05 The potential Rico Verhoeven fight 4:30 Why the “Francis was scared” argument doesn’t hold up 5:05 Why legacy talk is frustrating 5:30 The real reason Francis’ answer stood out Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Chael Addresses Dan Hooker Allegations
articles.description: Don’t sleep on @ultrapouches. New customers get 50% Off with code CHAEL at https://takeultra.com ! #UltraPouches #adBad Dan Hooker recently found himself in the middle of a rumor that had nothing to do with fighting. A story resurfaced involving a past personal relationship, and suddenly the MMA world was talking about gossip instead of fights. Hooker made the unusual decision to address it directly. According to Hooker, the situation involved a consensual arrangement with an adult, and the person involved later chose to speak publicly after the relationship ended. Chael believes Hooker handled the situation the right way — by telling the truth and addressing it head-on. But Chael also explains why stories like this can be dangerous for fighters, and why sometimes the smartest PR move is to say one final thing: "This is the last time I’m going to speak about this." Chael also reflects on a similar situation involving Brock Lesnar, and why letting time pass can sometimes be the only way for controversies like this to disappear. At the end of the day, Chael says the focus should be on what Dan Hooker is actually known for — fighting in the Octagon and continuing his climb toward a title opportunity. Because if we’re going to talk about Dan Hooker…it should probably be about fighting. Timestamps 0:00 The Dan Hooker rumor 0:40 The phone call Chael received 1:30 Why Chael didn’t care about the gossip 2:10 Dan Hooker’s public response 3:00 Why telling the truth matters 3:40 The Brock Lesnar comparison 4:20 The PR move Hooker should make 4:40 Why the focus should stay on fighting Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench

articles.title: Why the UFC Chose Strickland vs Chimaev (And What Happens to Imavov?)
articles.description: Sean Strickland vs Khamzat Chimaev is officially happening… but that raises a much bigger question. What happened to Nassourdine Imavov? For months the assumption inside the MMA world was that Imavov was next in line for the title shot at middleweight. He beat top contenders, nobody was calling him out, and the whispers inside the locker room suggested the fight was already locked in. Then suddenly the UFC announces Strickland vs Chimaev. So what changed? Chael breaks down: • Why the UFC pivoted away from Imavov• The strange clues that hinted this fight was coming• The rumors about Chimaev leaving middleweight• Why Vegas opened Chimaev as a 6-to-1 favorite• And why the odds might actually be wrong Plus — where Imavov goes next, and why Kyle Borralho may suddenly be in the most important fight of his career. 0:00 Strickland vs Chimaev Announced 0:50 Why Imavov Was Expected to Get the Fight 2:13 Chimaev Visa Rumors Explained 3:32 Strickland vs Chimaev Odds (6 to 1) 4:44 Can Strickland Stop Chimaev’s Wrestling? 6:05 Why Strickland Might Be the Right Opponent 6:40 What Happens to Imavov Now? articles.title: It’s Time to Bring Back Tsarukyan & Mokaev to the Octagon
articles.description: Arman Tsarukyan vs Muhammad Mokaev wasn’t just a grappling match — it was a statement. Two of the best young fighters in the sport found a way to keep their names relevant without fighting in the UFC, which is incredibly difficult to do. Muhammad Mokaev was 14-0 and ranked #3 in the world when the UFC released him. The promotion said he was “difficult to deal with,” something Mokaev says caught him completely off guard. Instead of disappearing, Mokaev has done the opposite. He’s taken wrestling matches in Russia, fights in other promotions, and now stepped in on short notice to grapple Arman Tsarukyan — giving up nearly 30 pounds in the process. Tsarukyan deserves credit too. Taking a match where the public believes you're supposed to win is never easy. If you win, you get no credit. If you lose, you hear about it forever. The result was a fast, chaotic grappling match before Tsarukyan eventually locked up the choke. But the real story isn’t who won. The story is that Mokaev is still competing everywhere he can — and Chael believes that deserves recognition. Sometimes the hardest thing in combat sports isn’t winning fights. It’s staying relevant when you’re not fighting in the UFC. And right now, Mokaev is doing everything he can to prove he belongs back. Timestamps 0:00 Two fighters keeping their name alive outside the UFC 0:20 The Mohammed Mokaev situation 1:00 Why the UFC released him 2:00 Mokaev’s response and accountability 3:30 How young fighters rely on managers 4:00 Taking matches anywhere he can 4:40 Sarukyan vs Mokaev grappling match 5:20 Giving up 20–30 pounds 6:00 Why Sarukyan was also in a tough spot 6:40 Why fights on Tuesday/Thursday go unnoticed 7:00 Why Mokaev deserves recognition Bad Guy Inc merch now available at https://BadGuyInc.com Click here to subscribe: https://bit.ly/3FrHIPK Follow me on IG: https://bit.ly/3HY7BIE Follow me on Twitter: https://bit.ly/3tvY5J7 Follow me on Threads https://www.threads.com/@sonnench
